element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic melee combat against early monsters
Dog can hold enemies for combo attacks; establishes companion mechanic

element_introduction_order.element: Secondary animal companion (penguin Chilly/Penguy with ice sword power-up)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Elemental sword enhancement; dual-ability equip system

element_introduction_order.element: Environmental puzzle solving via animal abilities
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Animals unlock previously inaccessible areas; gating mechanic
Story progression gate; triggers Part 2 transition

element_introduction_order.element: Human dialogue recovery / time travel narrative
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Reveals deeper story; world transformation mechanic
Gradual ability growth; player progression fantasy

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Two-act structure: Part 1 emphasizes exploration and gradual companion acquisition with mounting tension toward Dragon encounter; Part 2 shifts toward narrative depth and world restoration after midpoint climax

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Animal-gated areas; animals grant passage to previously inaccessible zones indicating optional/progression-locked content

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with basic sword combat; pet dog companion acquired early to establish mechanic
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increasing complexity via multi-animal ability combinations; environmental puzzles requiring specific animal powers; approach mirrors Zelda dungeon progression
difficulty_curve.late_game: Dragon boss as Part 1 climax; Part 2 shifts focus toward narrative and world mechanics rather than pure difficulty escalation

notable_level_patterns.description: New areas unlock as player acquires animals with specific abilities; classic Zelda-style ability-lock progression adapted to companion system
Part 1: Corona loses human speech, gains animal speech; Part 2: Corona regains human speech after Dragon defeat, enabling time-travel narrative. Mirrors internal growth arc.
SNES Zelda-inspired overhead perspective; exploration-driven with sword combat encounters and environmental logic puzzles
